briefs.title: Burnaby Council updates its own meeting procedure rules for 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed an amendment to its Procedure Bylaw 2024, updating the rules that govern how council meetings are run. This is an internal housekeeping change and does not directly regulate businesses.

briefs.title: Burnaby rezones 8265–8303 10th Avenue — land use rules at this address have changed
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has given final approval to a zoning amendment (a change to the rules about how land can be used or developed) for the properties at 8265–8303 10th Avenue.
briefs.action_required: If you own, lease, or plan to develop property at 8265–8303 10th Avenue, contact Burnaby Planning to confirm how the new zoning affects you.

briefs.title: Burnaby shelter at 3020 Gilmore cannot open until Good Neighbour and Operational Plans are approved
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted to block occupancy of the 3020 Gilmore diversion shelter until BC Housing and the operator submit a Good Neighbour Plan and an Operational Management Plan that satisfy the City. Council also asked BC Housing to run a new round of public consultation.
briefs.action_required: If you operate or own property near 3020 Gilmore, monitor City notices for the public re-engagement process and submit feedback.

briefs.title: Burnaby Bylaw Updated to Regulate Bear Spray — What Businesses Need to Know
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed an amendment to its Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 to add rules around bear spray. This means businesses in Burnaby may face new local rules about how bear spray is sold, stored, or handled.
briefs.action_required: Check with the City of Burnaby for details on any bear spray restrictions that apply to your business.

briefs.title: Burnaby raises municipal fees and charges — all businesses paying city fees affected
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially passed an amendment updating its Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw for 2026, meaning various city fees and charges have been formally approved and will take effect.
briefs.action_required: Check Burnaby's updated fee schedule to see if any fees you pay to the city have changed.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Zoning change approved for 7305 & 7317 Buller Ave — development may follow
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council passed the first two readings of a zoning bylaw amendment for two properties on Buller Avenue, moving the rezoning application one step closer to approval.
briefs.action_required: If you own or lease property near 7305–7317 Buller Ave, monitor future council readings for final approval.

briefs.title: Burnaby North Road BIA Bylaw 2026 approved — businesses in the area now affected
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed the Burnaby Business Improvement Area (BIA) Bylaw 2026 for the North Road area. A BIA is a defined business district where local businesses collectively fund area improvements and promotions — and are required to pay a levy (a mandatory fee added to your property tax bill).
briefs.action_required: Check if your business is within the Burnaby North Road BIA boundary and confirm your annual BIA levy amount.

briefs.title: Burnaby now regulates bear spray sales under its Business Licence Bylaw
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed Bylaw #14798, adding bear spray sales rules to the Burnaby Business Licence Bylaw 2017. Businesses that sell bear spray in Burnaby must now comply with these new licensing requirements.
briefs.action_required: Check if your Burnaby business sells bear spray and confirm you meet the new licence requirements.

briefs.title: Stoney Creek watershed development approvals may be paused pending staff review
briefs.summary: Council has sent a request from the Stoney Creek Environment Committee to staff for review. The request asks for new watershed protections and a pause on approving new development in the Stoney Creek area.
briefs.action_required: Monitor staff review outcomes if you have active or planned development projects near Stoney Creek watershed.

briefs.title: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw text amendment passes first and second reading
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved the first two readings of a zoning bylaw text amendment (a change to the written rules that control how land and buildings can be used). The specific rule changes have not been detailed in the available information.
briefs.action_required: Check Burnaby's zoning bylaw updates to see if your property or business use is affected.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: 49-Storey Mixed-Use Tower at Springer Avenue Moves Forward with Rezoning and Land Sale Approval
briefs.summary: City council approved drafting a rezoning bylaw (a rule change that allows a new type of building on a property) for a 49-storey residential and retail tower at 2209 & 2225 Springer Avenue, and agreed in principle to sell city-owned land for the project.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near Springer Avenue, monitor rezoning progress as retail space and foot traffic in the area may change.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: City approves 2026 active transportation education and promotion programmes
briefs.summary: Council has approved a set of education and promotion initiatives for 2026 focused on active transportation (walking, cycling, and other non-motorized travel). No new rules or compliance requirements for businesses were introduced.

briefs.title: Stoney Creek Construction Disruption Rules Under Feasibility Review by Municipal Staff
briefs.summary: City council has sent recommendations from the Environment Committee about reducing construction disruptions near Stoney Creek to staff for a feasibility review. No new rules are in place yet — staff will report back before any changes are made.

briefs.title: 20-Storey Rental Tower at 5058 Imperial Street Moves Toward Rezoning Approval
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved moving forward with a rezoning bylaw for a 20-storey mixed-use market rental building at 5058 Imperial Street. The bylaw will now proceed to first and second reading, meaning it is still in early stages of approval.

briefs.title: Burnaby's Blake Village rezoning moves forward — taller buildings and new open space rules proposed
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved the first step to rezone 6800 Lougheed Highway, updating the Blake Village Master Plan with new rules for building height, size, and open space to align with the city's long-term growth plan (OCP 2050).
briefs.action_required: If your business is near 6800 Lougheed Highway, monitor future public hearings to submit feedback.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Heritage Alteration Permit granted for chimney work at 6490 Deer Lake Avenue, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved a Heritage Alteration Permit allowing chimney alterations at the W.J. Mathers House ('Altnadene') at 6490 Deer Lake Avenue. This permit is required before any changes can be made to a designated heritage property.

briefs.title: On-Street Public Parking Fees Increased by City Council
briefs.summary: City council has approved an increase to on-street public pay parking rates. If you or your customers rely on street parking near your business, expect to pay more at the meter.
briefs.action_required: Check the new parking rates and inform customers who regularly use on-street parking near your business.

briefs.title: City-owned EV charging stations switch to energy-based fees under new bylaw amendments
briefs.summary: The City has approved a new fee structure for its public EV charging stations, charging users based on energy consumed (kilowatt-hours) instead of time parked. Supporting bylaws have been updated to reflect this change.
briefs.action_required: If your business uses or manages City EV charging stations, review the new energy-based fee schedule now.
briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Municipal council renews $162,400 portable toilet contract with Jiffy John Rentals
briefs.summary: Council approved a contract renewal with Jiffy John Rentals Ltd. to supply portable rental toilets at an estimated cost of $162,400 including taxes.

briefs.title: City renews $670,000 security contract with Guardteck for one more year
briefs.summary: City council approved a one-year renewal of its security officer services contract with Guardteck Security Corp, covering city buildings and special events, valued at approximately $670,000 including taxes.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council approves Option B design for Landmark Burnaby Sign
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved the Option B design for the Landmark Burnaby Sign and directed staff to move forward with the project.

briefs.title: Up to 84 outdoor events approved at Deer Lake, Central Park, and Swangard Stadium in 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved up to 84 outdoor events at Deer Lake Park, Central Park, and Swangard Stadium from March 15 to November 1, 2026, under the city's Noise or Sound Abatement Bylaw.
briefs.action_required: If you plan to host or vend at outdoor events at these venues in 2026, confirm your event falls within the approved schedule.

key_dates.label: Burnaby outdoor event season opens — Deer Lake, Central Park, Swangard Stadium
Burnaby outdoor event season closes — Deer Lake, Central Park, Swangard Stadium

briefs.title: Antenna variance permit at 9001 University Crescent sent back for further staff review
briefs.summary: A permit application to allow antenna equipment that exceeds normal size and height limits at 9001 University Crescent has been sent back to city staff for more review before a decision is made.

briefs.title: Commercial fleet parking permit renewed at 7000 Lougheed Highway
briefs.summary: Council approved the renewal of a Temporary Use Permit (a time-limited permission to use land in a way not normally allowed by zoning) for commercial fleet vehicle parking and rentals at 7000 Lougheed Highway.

briefs.title: Burnaby freezes $63,005 FCM membership dues until Big City Mayors' Caucus seat secured
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has paused its 2026–2027 membership payment of $63,005 to the Federation of Canadian Municipalities (FCM) until the city is given a seat on the Big City Mayors' Caucus, a group that gives large cities a direct voice in national policy discussions.

briefs.title: Utility access right granted over 3860 Sperling Avenue for Burnaby Lake Recreation Centre
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council approved a statutory right-of-way (a legal permission allowing utility companies to access and use part of a property) over a section of 3860 Sperling Avenue to support utility work for the Burnaby Lake Recreation Centre project.

briefs.title: Antenna variance application at 8888 University Drive sent back for further staff review
briefs.summary: A permit application to allow antenna equipment to exceed normal size and height limits at 8888 University Drive has been sent back to city staff for more review before a decision is made.

briefs.title: Burnaby now requires a business licence to sell bear spray — new rules take effect
briefs.summary: Burnaby has amended its Business Licence Bylaw and Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw to regulate the sale of bear spray. Retailers selling bear spray in Burnaby must now meet new licensing conditions or face fines.
briefs.action_required: Check if your Burnaby business licence covers bear spray sales and update it if required.

briefs.title: Lower Westridge construction permits paused; city-wide natural hazard assessment program launched
briefs.summary: City council launched a Natural Hazard Development Permit Area program and approved $120,000 to assess hazard risks in Lower Westridge. New construction permits in Lower Westridge have been put on hold until the assessment is complete.
briefs.action_required: If you plan to build in Lower Westridge, expect permit delays until the hazard assessment is finished.

briefs.title: Burnaby North Road BIA renewed for 2026–2031 with new bylaw coming
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved a five-year renewal of the North Road Business Improvement Association (BIA — a group that collects levies from local businesses to fund area improvements) for 2026–2031 and directed staff to draft a new supporting bylaw.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business is in the North Road BIA zone — a levy may apply to you starting 2026.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA renewal term begins
Burnaby North Road BIA renewal term ends

briefs.title: Plumbing Permit Fees Changing — Check New Costs Before Your Next Project
briefs.summary: City council approved small changes to plumbing permit fees and directed staff to write a new bylaw to put those changes into effect. If you pull plumbing permits, your costs may shift.
briefs.action_required: Check the updated fee schedule before applying for your next plumbing permit to budget accurately.

briefs.title: City will now remove outdated land restrictions from private properties on request
briefs.summary: The City has approved the removal of old restrictive covenants (legal rules tied to a property that limit how it can be used) that it previously placed on private land. Property owners can now ask the City Solicitor to formally remove these outdated restrictions.
briefs.action_required: If your property has a City-imposed restrictive covenant, contact the City Solicitor's office to request its removal.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
